channel.title: Isaiah on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Isaiah on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Isaiah 1
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 1

Conversational Yahveh Covenant

1 Visions Isaiah Ben-Amoz Saw

About Judah and Jerusalem

During the Reigns of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz,

and Hezekiah, Kings of Judah

Rebellious Judah

2 Listen, earth and sky, Yahveh says,

                              “I brought up sons,

                                    but they’ve rebelled.

                                             3An ox knows its owner,

                                    and a donkey recognizes its owner’s manger,

item.title: Isaiah 2
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 2

Yahveh’s Future Reign

1 A Message Isaiah Ben-Amoz Saw About Judah and Jerusalem

2 In the last days, the mountain where Yahveh’s Temple is,

            will become the tallest mountain;

      it will rise above the hills,

            and nations everywhere will stream into it.

3 Many nationalities will say,

      “Let’s go up to Yahveh’s mountain,

item.title: Isaiah 3
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 3

Judgment Against Judah

1 The Lord Yahveh of Hosts will remove

food and water from Judah and Jerusalem,

2 strong men and soldiers, judges and prophets,

      fortunetellers and elders,

3 captains of 50 and their men,

      advisors, expert magicians, and skilled astrologers.

4 “I’ll make boys their officials,

item.title: Isaiah 4
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 4

Yahveh’s Branch

1 At that time, seven widows will grab hold of one man and say, “We’ll provide our own food and clothes. Just let us take your name so we won’t be old maids!”

2 But at that time, Yahveh’s branch will be beautiful and glorious. What the ground produces will be the pride and joy of Israel’s survivors. 3 The remnant in Zion and Jerusalem will be holy, everybody that’s in his record books for living there. 4 When Yahveh has washed the grime off of Zion’s inhabitants and cleaned up the bloodstains in Jerusalem by the spirit of judgment and burning, 5 then he’ll create over Mount Zion and its assemblies a cloud of smoke during the day, a bright blazing fire at night, and a canopy above the glory. 6 It’ll be shade from daytime heat and shelter from storms and rain.

item.title: Isaiah 5
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 5

The Song of the Vineyard

1 For the one I love, let me sing a song about his vineyard:

                        The one I love had a vineyard on a fertile hill.

                        2 He spaded up the ground, picked up the rocks,

                              and planted the best grapevines in it;

                        he built a tower there

item.title: Isaiah 6
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 6

Isaiah’s Call and Cleansing

1 The year King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a high throne, and his train filled the Temple. 2 Seraphs hovered above him, each with six wings:  two covered their faces, two covered their feet, and with two they flew. 3 One shouted to the other,       

                        “Holy, Holy, Holy is Yahveh of Hosts;

item.title: Isaiah 7
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 7

A Message for Ahaz

1 At the time of Ahaz Ben-Jotham Ben-Uzziah, king of Judah, Rezin, king of Syria, and Pekah Ben-Remaliah, king of Israel, attacked Jerusalem but couldn’t take it. 2 When the news reached Judah’s royal court that Syria was in league with Ephraim, their hearts sank, and they shook like trees in the wind.

3 Yahveh said to Isaiah,

item.title: Isaiah 8
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 8

Assyria’s Coming Invasion

1 Then Yahveh told me, “Take a large tablet and etch on it in standard letters ‘Maher-shalal-hash-baz’ [quick to the plunder, swift to the spoil].” 2 I got reputable men to witness it: Uriah the priest and Zechariah Ben-Jeberechiah. 3 So I had relations with my wife the prophetess, and she had a son. Yahveh told me,

item.title: Isaiah 9
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 9

A Messiah Has Been Born to Us

1 But there won’t be any more darkness for those who have been in distress. In earlier times, God didn’t give much prominence to the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali, but at a future time he’ll make it glorious along the road from the Jordan along the Sea of Galilee, Galilee of the Gentiles.

                        2 Those who walk in deep darkness

item.title: Isaiah 10
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 10

                  10:1Misfortune awaits judges that enact unfair laws

                                    and render illegal verdicts,

                              2 who deprive the needy of justice

                                    and rob the poor of their rights

                                          so they can make widows their spoil

                                                and orphans their plunder.

                              3 What are you going to do when punishment arrives,

item.title: Isaiah 11
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 11

A Branch from Jesse

                  11:1A sapling will grow out of Jesse’s stump;

                              a branch from his roots will bear fruit.

                        2 Yahveh’s spirit will rest on him,

                              the spirit of wisdom and insight,

                                    counsel and strength,

                                    knowledge and respect for Yahveh.

item.title: Isaiah 12
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 12

A Song of Praise for Salvation

1 At that time, you’ll say,

“Thank you, Yahveh;    

though you were angry with me,                               

                              you turned your anger away

                              and reassured me.

                                    2You’re my salvation;

                              I’ll trust you and not be afraid,

                              because you’re my strength and song.”

item.title: Isaiah 13
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 13

1 Oracle About Babylon That Isaiah Ben-Amoz Saw

2 “Hoist a signal flag on a bare hilltop; call out an army

      and signal them to attack the mansions of the nobles.

3 I’ve commanded my consecrated ones

      and strong men to carry out my anger.”                         

Is 13:1-3

4 There’s noise in the mountains like a large army,

item.title: Isaiah 14
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 14

A Taunt for the King of Babylon

1 When Yahveh will have mercy on Jacob, choose Israel again, and settle them in their own land, foreigners will join Jacob’s house. 2 The nations will help bring them home. Israel will possess them as an inheritance in Yahveh’s land, as male and female servants. They’ll capture their captors and rule their rulers. 3 When Yahveh gives them relief from their pain, turmoil, and harsh slavery, 4 they’ll taunt the king of Babylon:

item.title: Isaiah 15
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 15

1 Oracle About Moab

                        Moab will be ruined

                              because Ar and Kir will be wiped out overnight.

                        2 The people of Dibon will go up to their temple

                              and high places to weep.

                        Moab will mourn over Nebo and Medeba;

                              everyone’s head will be bald, and their beard shaved off.

item.title: Isaiah 16
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 16

1 Send tribute lambs to the ruler of the land,

                              from Sela through the desert to Mount Zion.

                        2 At the Arnon fords the women of Moab will be

                              left fluttering like birds or homeless nestlings.

Is 16:1-2

                        3 “Give us advice, grant us justice;

item.title: Isaiah 17
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 17

1 Oracle About Damascus

                        “Damascus will stop being a city,

                              and become a pile of ruins.         

                        2 The villages around Aroer will be deserted;

                              flocks will bed down in them,

                                    with no one to chase them away.

                        3 Fortified towns will disappear from Ephraim,

item.title: Isaiah 18
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 18

An Oracle About Nubia

1 Ah, land of fluttering wings,

                        land beyond the headwaters of the Nile in Nubia,

                        2 that sends envoys by sea in papyrus boats—

                        go swiftly, ambassadors, to a nation tall and smooth,

                        a people feared far and wide,

                        a powerful, oppressive nation

item.title: Isaiah 19
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 19

1 Oracle About Egypt

                        Yahveh is riding a swift cloud to Egypt;

                              Egypt’s idols tremble in his presence;

                              the Egyptians’ hearts melt.

                        2 “I’ll pit the Egyptians against one another,

                              brother against brother, neighbor against neighbor;

                                    city against city, district against district.

item.title: Isaiah 20
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 20

A Sign Against Egypt and Nubia

1 Sargon, king of Assyria, sent his general to attack Ashdod. 2 That year Yahveh spoke through Isaiah Ben-Amoz, “Take the burlap off your waist and remove your sandals.” So he went around naked and barefoot. 3 Yahveh said,

“My servant Isaiah has gone naked and barefoot for three years as an omen to Egypt and Nubia. 4 The king of Assyria will lead captives away from there, young and old, naked and barefoot with their butts showing, to Egypt’s humiliation. 5 People will be disgraced and disappointed by Nubia their hope and Egypt their pride. 6 The people along the coast will say, ‘Look at what’s happened to them! How can we escape who fled there for protection from the king of Assyria?!’”

item.title: Isaiah 21
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 21

1 Oracle About the Sandy Wastes Beside the Sea

                        Like a haboob sweeping in from the Negev,

                              catastrophe will sweep in through the desert from a terrible land.

                        2 I see a harsh vision:

                                    plunderers plundering; destroyers destroying.

                                    Go on, Elam; lay siege, Media.

item.title: Isaiah 22
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 22

1 Oracle About the Valley of Vision

What’s the matter with you?

Why are you all running up to the housetops?

                        2 You noisy, exuberant city in an uproar,

                              your dead weren’t slain in battle,

                                    but by famine and disease.

                              3 Your rulers all fled together

item.title: Isaiah 23
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 23

1 Oracle About Tyre

Wail, you merchant ships of Tarshish,

      because Tyre’s destroyed without home or harbor,

      just like the rumor you heard all the way over at Cyprus.

2 Be quiet, you people on the coast, merchants of Sidon;

      your traders crossed the sea

                  3and sailed on many bodies of water.

item.title: Isaiah 24
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 24

Devastation of the Earth

1 Yahveh is laying the earth waste, defacing its surface, scattering its residents. 2 People and priests, servants and masters, maids and mistresses, buyers and sellers, lenders and borrowers, creditors and debtors—everyone will be affected. 3 The earth will be completely despoiled because Yahveh said so. 4 The earth withers, and its people waste away, even its high-ranking ones, 5 because they’ve polluted it by sinning against God’s laws, statutes, and everlasting covenant. 6 So a curse devastates the earth, and its people have to pay for their guilt. They’re burned; there aren’t many left.

item.title: Isaiah 25
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 25

Praise for Judgment and Salvation

1 Yahveh, you’re my God.

                        I’ll honor and thank you

                              for doing the wonderful things you planned long ago.

                        2 You’ve reduced fortified towns to ruble;

                              the foreigners’ palaces will never be rebuilt.

                        3 So strong people will honor you;

item.title: Isaiah 26
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 26

A Song of Praise to the LORD

1 At that time, they’ll sing a song in Judah:

                        We have an impregnable city;           

                              it’s set up with secure walls and ramparts.

                        2 Open the gates so a good nation can enter,

                              one that stays faithful.

item.title: Isaiah 27
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 27

1 At that time, Yahveh will punish Leviathan with his fierce, big strong sword, Leviathan the swift, slithering snake. He’ll kill the dragon that’s in the sea.

2 Then,

                        “There’ll be a delightful vineyard to sing about!

                         3 I, Yahveh, will be its keeper;

                              I’ll water it constantly

item.title: Isaiah 28
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 28

An Oracle About Samaria

                  28:1Misfortune awaits the proud crown of Ephraim’s drunks,

                             the fading flower of its glorious beauty,

Samaria,

that’s at the head of the fertile valley

                                          of those drunk with wine.

                        2 Yahveh has a strong and powerful military agent;

                                                   he’ll throw Samaria down on the ground

item.title: Isaiah 29
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 29

An Oracle About Jerusalem

                       29:1“Misfortune awaits you, Ariel, city where David camped!

                                    Year after year, you observe the festivals on schedule.

                              2 Yet I’ll bring disaster on you, Ariel;      

                                    you’ll lament and mourn;

                                    you’ll be to me what Ariel means [blood-covered altar].

item.title: Isaiah 30
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 30

No Help from Egypt

                  30:1Yahveh says, “Misfortune awaits rebellious children

                                    that carry out plans, but not mine,

                                    that make alliances, but not by my Spirit,

                                          and thereby pile sin on top of error;

                                    2 children that go down to Egypt to get help instead of consulting me,

item.title: Isaiah 31
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 31

Warning to Those Who Rely on Egypt

                  31:1Misfortune lies ahead for those who go down to Egypt for help,

                              who rely on horses,

                              that trust in chariots because they are many

                                    and in horsemen because they are strong,

                              instead of looking to the Holy One of Israel,

item.title: Isaiah 32
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 32

Israel’s Ultimate Deliverance

                  32:1A righteous king is coming;

                                    his princes will rule honestly.

                        2 Each will provide a refuge from the wind,

                                    a shelter from the storm,

                                    a stream of water in the desert,

                                    the shade of a big tree in parched land.

item.title: Isaiah 33
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 33

An Oracle About Assyria

                  33:1Misfortune awaits you, destroyers

                                    who haven’t been destroyed yourselves;

                              misfortune awaits you, treacherous people

                                    that others haven’t treated treacherously.

                              As soon as you’re done destroying, you’ll be destroyed;

                                    as soon as you’ve finished your treachery,

                                          others will deal treacherously with you.

item.title: Isaiah 34
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 34

An Oracle for the Nations

                  34:1Come close, nations, and listen!

                       world and everything in it, pay attention.

                        2 Yahveh is furious with the nations and their armies;

                              he’ll destroy them and deliver them to slaughter.

                        3 Their slain will be thrown out;

item.title: Isaiah 35
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 35

Joy for the Redeemed

1-2 The desert wilderness will be happy;

                              the Arabah will blossom profusely with crocuses,

                              and sing and celebrate.

                        It will be as glorious as Lebanon,

                              as majestic as Carmel and Sharon.

                        They’ll see the glory of Yahveh,

                              the majesty of our God.

item.title: Isaiah 36
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 36

Sennacherib Invades Judah

1 In the fourteenth year of King Hezekiah, Sennacherib king of Assyria attacked the fortified towns in Judah and took them. 2 He sent the Rabshakeh from Lachish to Jerusalem with a large army. They took position by the aqueduct to the upper pool on the road to the Fuller’s Field. 3 Eliakim Ben-Hilkiah, the palace administrator; Shebna, the court secretary; and Joah Ben-Asaph, the royal historian, went out to meet them.

item.title: Isaiah 37
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 37

Hezekiah Seeks Isaiah’s Help

1 When Hezekiah heard it, he tore his clothes, put on burlap, and went to the Temple. 2 He sent Eliakim, Shebna, and the senior priests to the prophet Isaiah. 3 They went to him and said,

“Hezekiah says, ‘This is a distressing, discouraging, dejecting day. Babies have come due, but there’s no strength to deliver them. 4 Maybe Yahveh your God has heard the Rabshakeh that the king of Assyria sent to mock the living God. Maybe he’ll reprove him for it. Pray for the remnant that’s left.’” [ 5 ]

item.title: Isaiah 38
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 38

Hezekiah’s Sickness and Recovery

1 Around that time, Hezekiah got deathly ill, and Isaiah came to tell him, “Yahveh says to set your house in order; you’re going to die.”

2 Hezekiah turned to the wall and prayed, 3 “Yahwh, please remember how I’ve followed you honestly with all my heart and done what you consider good.” He cried bitterly.

item.title: Isaiah 39
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 39

Envoys from Babylon

1 Soon after this, Merodach-baladan Ben-Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present to Hezekiah because he’d heard he’d been sick and had recovered. 2 That pleased Hezekiah, and he showed the ambassadors everything in his treasure house, the silver and gold, the spices and precious oil, his whole armory, and everything in his royal treasuries. There wasn’t anything in his palace or kingdom that he didn’t show them.

item.title: Isaiah 40
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 40

Comfort for God’s People

                  40:1“Comfort, comfort my people,” your God says.

                                             2“Speak graciously to Jerusalem;

                                    tell it that its warfare’s over,

                                          its iniquity’s removed,

                                          it’s received from Yahveh

                                                double for its sins.”                                       

Is 40:1-2

                                    3 A voice is calling,

item.title: Isaiah 41
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 41

God’s Help for Israel

                  41:1“Lands beyond the sea, come to order and listen;

                                          prepare your case;

                                    step forward and present it;

                                          your case is next on the docket.

                              2 Who has stirred up the king in the east

                                          that he rightly called to his service,

item.title: Isaiah 42
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 42

The Servant of Yahveh

                  42:1“My Servant that I uphold,

                                          my Chosen One I delight in,

                                    I’ve put my Spirit on him;                  

                                          he’ll bring righteousness to the Gentiles.

                              2 He won’t holler or be boisterous;

                                    he won’t make a racket in the streets.

                              3 He won’t break a bruised reed

item.title: Isaiah 43
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 43

Israel’s Only Savior

                  43:1But this is what Yahveh your creator says, Jacob,

                                    the One that formed you, Israel,

                                    “You don’t need to be afraid, because I’ve redeemed you

                                          and called you by name. You’re mine!

                                    2 When you pass through the sea, I’m with you;

item.title: Isaiah 44
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 44

                       44:1“But listen now, Jacob my servant,

                                    Israel my chosen one.

                              2 This is what Yahveh says who created you

                                    and formed you from the womb,

                                    who will help you:

                                    ‘Don’t be afraid, Jacob my servant,

                                          Jeshurun my chosen one.

                              3 As I’ll pour out water on thirsty ground

item.title: Isaiah 45
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 45

Cyrus, Yahveh’s Chosen One

                  45:1This is what Yahveh says to Cyrus his anointed

                                    whose right hand he’ll empower                   

                                          to subdue nations and dethrone kings,

                                          to open fortress gates so they won’t be shut:

                              2 “I’ll go ahead of you to level out the mountains,

item.title: Isaiah 46
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 46

Babylon’s False Gods

1 Bel bows down, Nebo stoops over;

                              their images, loaded on beasts of burden and livestock,

                                    are too heavy for their tired animals to bear.

                              2 The idols themselves are stooped over;

                                    their owners are bowed down too;

                                    the people couldn’t keep the burden

item.title: Isaiah 47
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 47

Prediction of the Fall of Babylon

                  47:1“Come sit in the dirt,

                                          virgin daughter of Babylon.

                                    Sit on the ground without a throne;

                                          you won’t be treated honorably anymore.

                              2 Grind meal with heavy millstones;

                                    take off your veil, strip off your skirt,

item.title: Isaiah 48
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 48

Stubborn Israel

                  48:1“Listen to this, Jacob called ‘Israel,’

                                    that came from Judah’s family,

                                    that swear by Yahveh and invoke Israel’s God,

                                          but not sincerely and rightly,

                                    2 that call yourself The Holy City

                                          and depend on Yahveh of Hosts:

Is 48:1-2

                              3 long ago, I told you what was going to happen;

item.title: Isaiah 49
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 49

The Servant of the LORD

                  49:1Listen, far off lands;

                                                pay attention, distant nations.

                                          Yahveh called me before I was born;

                                    he named me inside my mother’s body.

                        2 He made my mouth like a sharp sword

                                    and hid me under his hand;

item.title: Isaiah 50
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 50

Israel’s Sin and the Servant’s Obedience

                  50:1Yahveh says,

                              “Did I put your mother away with divorce papers?

                                    Did I sell you as slaves to pay my debts?

                              No, I sold you because of your transgressions

                                    and divorced your mother for her sins.

                              2 When I came, why wasn’t anybody home?

item.title: Isaiah 51
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 51

Everlasting Salvation for Israel

                  51:1“Listen to me, you that want to be good,

                                    that look to Yahveh,

                                          the Rock you were hewn from,

                                          the quarry you came from.

                        2 Look to Abraham your ancestor,

                                    to Sarah who bore you in pain;

                              he was just one man when I called him;

item.title: Isaiah 52
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 52

Deliverance for Jerusalem

1 Wake up, wake up;

                              dress yourself with strength, Zion;

                                    put on your beautiful clothes, Jerusalem the holy city;

                              defiled, godless people won’t invade you anymore.

                                    2Get up, shake off the dust, captive Jerusalem;

                              take the chains off your neck, resident in Zion,

item.title: Isaiah 53
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 53

1 Who has believed our message?

                        Who has Yahveh shown his arm to?

                        He grew up before Yahveh like a tender sprout,

                              like a shoot out of parched ground.

                        2 He wasn’t particularly handsome or noble

                              to draw our attention to him

item.title: Isaiah 54
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 54

The Future Glory for Zion

                  54:1“Shout for joy, childless woman;

                              break into joyful shouting and cry out,

                                    you that have never had birth pangs;

                              because the woman that’s by herself

                                    will have more children than a married woman,”

                                                                        Yahveh says.

                        2 “Make a bigger space for your tent;

item.title: Isaiah 55
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 55

Invitation to Yahveh’s Salvation

1 “Everyone that’s thirsty, come to the water;

                              you that don’t have any money, come, buy, and eat;

                              get wine and milk for free.

                        2 Why spend money on what’s not food,

                              wages on what doesn’t satisfy?

                        3 Come and listen so you can live;

item.title: Isaiah 56
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 56

Blessings for All Nations

1 Yahveh says,

                        “Maintain righteousness and be godly

                              because my salvation’s coming;

                                    my deliverance will arrive.

                        2 They are fortunate who do that,

                              who hold onto it,

                              who honor my Sabbath

                                    and don’t do anything sinful.

item.title: Isaiah 57
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 57

1 Good people perish and nobody notices;

                        the devout disappear without anyone realizing it,

                              because God removes good people out of harm’s way.

                        2 They who take the godly road

                              go to peace and rest in their graves.        

Idolatrous Worship Condemned

                        3 “But come here, you sons of sorcerers,

item.title: Isaiah 58
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 58

False and True Worship

1 “Shout; don’t hold back;

                              raise your voice like a ram’s horn;

                               tell my people their transgressions.

                        2 They look to me daily and seem to delight to know my ways,

                                    as if they’re a good nation that hasn’t left my ordinances;

                              they ask for favorable verdicts

item.title: Isaiah 59
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 59

Warnings Against Sin

                        1 It’s not that Yahveh’s hand is not too weak to save

                                    or his ear too deaf to hear you call.

                        2 It’s your iniquities that have separated you from him;

                              your sins have shut him off from you,

                                    so he ignores you.

item.title: Isaiah 60
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 60

Israel’s Future Glory

1 “Arise, shine; your light has come;

                              the glory of Yahveh has risen on you.

                        2 Darkness will cover the earth;

                                    thick blackness will blanket the nations;

                              but Yahveh will shine on you;

                                    his glory will appear on you.

                        3 Nations will come to your light,

item.title: Isaiah 61
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 61

The Year of Yahveh’s Favor

1 The Spirit of the Lord Yahveh is on me;

                              he’s anointed me to bring good news to the downtrodden.

                        He’s sent me to bandage up the brokenhearted,

                              to liberate captives,

                              to free prisoners,

    2 to announce that this is

item.title: Isaiah 62
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 62

Isaiah’s Prayer for Jerusalem

                        1 For Zion’s sake I’ll speak to God;

                                    for Jerusalem’s sake I won’t keep still,

                              until your goodness glows brightly,

                                    and your salvation radiates like a burning torch;

                              2 so the nations can witness your goodness

                                    and kings can gaze at your glory.

item.title: Isaiah 63
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 63

Vengeance Against Israel’s Enemies

                  63:1Who is this that’s coming from Edom

                                    in crimson clothes from Bozrah,

                                          one majestic in attire

                                          and marching with such power?

                              “The One that’s announcing vindication,

                                          that has the strength to save.”

                              2 Why are your clothes stained red

item.title: Isaiah 64
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 64

                  64:1Oh that you’d open the sky and come down

                                    and the mountains would quake in your presence—

                                          2 like a flame that burns brush and boils water—

                                    to show yourself to your adversaries

                                          so they’d tremble in your presence!

                              3 When you did awesome things we didn’t expect,

item.title: Isaiah 65
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 65

Judgment and Final Salvation

                  65:1“I let people look for me who weren’t asking about me;

                                    people that weren’t looking for me found me.

                              I said, ‘I’m here! Over here!’

                                    to people that didn’t call on me.

                              2 All day long I spread out my hands to a rebellious nation

item.title: Isaiah 66
item.description:
Isaiah Chapter 66

Judgment and Hope

                  66:1Yahveh says,

                              “Heaven is my throne; earth is my footstool;

                                    where are you going to build me a Temple to rest in?

                              2 I made all this;

                                          that’s why it’s here.                     

                                    But the ones I especially love
